name: Teri Thornton
abstract: Teri Thornton (born Shirley Enid Avery, September 1, 1934 – May 2, 2000) was an American jazz singer, music composer and pianist. She was known for recording several albums in the 1960's with a career spanning over four decades and composing several songs.

concerts.label: Afternoon at Carnegie Hall to Benefit Music for the Blind
Twilight at Carnegie Hall to Benefit Music for the Blind

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Teri Thornton who played piano and sang, she got the I think the National Endowment one year and a couple of years later she was gone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010
